mutex.h(3) Library Functions Manual mutex.h(3)

NAME

mutex.h - Per-thread mutex lock stack for deadlock detection.

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct mutex_stack_entry_t

Enumerations


enum mutex_stack_state_t { MUTEX_STACK_STATE_PENDING, MUTEX_STACK_STATE_LOCKED }

Functions


void mutex_stack_push_pending (uintptr_t mutex_key, const char *mutex_name)
Push a mutex onto the current thread's lock stack (PENDING state). void mutex_stack_mark_locked (uintptr_t mutex_key)
Mark the top of the current thread's lock stack as LOCKED (transitions PENDING -> LOCKED). void mutex_stack_pop (uintptr_t mutex_key)
Pop the top mutex from the current thread's lock stack (unlock). int mutex_stack_get_current (mutex_stack_entry_t *out_entries, int max_entries)
Get the current thread's lock stack. int mutex_stack_get_all_threads (mutex_stack_entry_t ***out_stacks, int **out_stack_counts, int *out_thread_count)
Get all threads' lock stacks for deadlock analysis. void mutex_stack_free_all_threads (mutex_stack_entry_t **stacks, int *stack_counts, int thread_count)
Free memory allocated by mutex_stack_get_all_threads(). void mutex_stack_detect_deadlocks (void)
Detect and log circular wait deadlocks. int mutex_stack_init (void)
Initialize mutex stack system. void mutex_stack_cleanup (void)
Cleanup mutex stack system. void mutex_stack_cleanup_current_thread (void)
Cleanup TLS stack for current thread Explicitly frees the thread-local mutex stack. Used to prevent leaks when TLS destructors might not run reliably (e.g., before TLS key deletion).

Detailed Description

Per-thread mutex lock stack for deadlock detection.

Maintains a stack of mutexes per thread, tracking both locked and pending locks. Used to detect circular wait deadlock patterns by comparing stacks across threads.

Definition in file debug/mutex.h.

Enumeration Type Documentation

enum mutex_stack_state_t

Lock state in the stack

Enumerator

MUTEX_STACK_STATE_PENDING
MUTEX_STACK_STATE_LOCKED

Definition at line 22 of file debug/mutex.h.

22              {
23   MUTEX_STACK_STATE_PENDING, // Attempting to acquire lock
24   MUTEX_STACK_STATE_LOCKED,  // Lock successfully acquired
25 } mutex_stack_state_t;










Function
  Documentation




void
  mutex_stack_cleanup (void )


Cleanup mutex stack system.


Definition at line 742 of file debug/mutex.c.


742                                {
743   // Signal shutdown to prevent new allocations from threads still running
744   atomic_store_bool(&g_shutting_down, true);
745 
746   // Manually free all stacks in the registry
747   // This must happen BEFORE deleting the TLS key to avoid double-free
748   // (destructor won't run on still-running threads until they exit, by which time
749   // these stacks will already be freed)
750   int count = atomic_load_int(&g_thread_registry_count);
751   for (int i = 0; i < count; i++) {
752     if (g_thread_registry[i].stack) {
753       // Use raw free() - stacks are allocated with raw malloc(), not SAFE_CALLOC()
754       free(g_thread_registry[i].stack);
755       g_thread_registry[i].stack = NULL;
756     }
757   }
758 
759   // Now delete the TLS key - destructors will see NULL in registry and skip freeing
760   // For any threads that haven't been registered yet, destructor will free directly
761   if (atomic_load_bool(&g_tls_initialized)) {
762     ascii_tls_key_delete(g_tls_mutex_stack);
763     atomic_store_bool(&g_tls_initialized, false);
764   }
765 
766   // Clear registry on cleanup using atomic operations
767   atomic_store_int(&g_thread_registry_count, 0);
768 }






References ascii_tls_key_delete(),
    atomic_load_bool(), atomic_load_int(),
    atomic_store_bool(), and atomic_store_int().


Referenced by asciichat_shared_destroy().






void
  mutex_stack_cleanup_current_thread (void )


Cleanup TLS stack for current thread Explicitly frees the
    thread-local mutex stack. Used to prevent leaks when TLS destructors might
    not run reliably (e.g., before TLS key deletion).


Definition at line 711 of file debug/mutex.c.


711                                               {
712   // Explicitly free the current thread's TLS stack
713   // This is used to prevent leaks when TLS destructors might not run reliably
714   // (e.g., debug threads exiting before mutex_stack_cleanup() deletes the TLS key)
715 
716   if (!atomic_load_bool(&g_tls_initialized)) {
717     return; // TLS not initialized, nothing to clean up
718   }
719 
720   thread_lock_stack_t *stack = (thread_lock_stack_t *)ascii_tls_get(g_tls_mutex_stack);
721   if (!stack) {
722     return; // No stack allocated for this thread
723   }
724 
725   // Clear from TLS
726   ascii_tls_set(g_tls_mutex_stack, NULL);
727 
728   // Update registry if this thread is registered
729   thread_id_t current_thread = asciichat_thread_self();
730   int count = atomic_load_int(&g_thread_registry_count);
731   for (int i = 0; i < count; i++) {
732     if (asciichat_thread_equal(g_thread_registry[i].thread_id, current_thread) && g_thread_registry[i].stack == stack) {
733       g_thread_registry[i].stack = NULL; // Mark as freed in registry
734       break;
735     }
736   }
737 
738   // Free the stack (raw free to match raw malloc above)
739   free(stack);
740 }






References ascii_tls_get(), ascii_tls_set(),
    asciichat_thread_equal(), asciichat_thread_self(),
    atomic_load_bool(), atomic_load_int(), and
  thread_id.


Referenced by debug_sync_final_cleanup().






void
  mutex_stack_detect_deadlocks (void )


Detect and log circular wait deadlocks. Analyzes all thread lock
    stacks to find circular wait patterns:


    
  
  • Thread A holds mutex1 and waits for mutex2
    • 
  
  • Thread B holds mutex2 and waits for mutex1
    • 



Logs detailed deadlock information with thread IDs, mutex names,
    and stack traces. Called periodically by debug thread (~100ms interval).




Detect and log circular wait deadlocks.




Detects both same-thread and multi-thread deadlock patterns of any
    length:




Same-thread deadlock:



  
•

  
Thread tries to acquire a mutex it already holds (recursive lock on
      non-recursive mutex)




Multi-thread circular wait (2-way, 3-way, N-way):


    
  
  • Uses DFS to detect cycles in the "waits-for" graph
    • 
  
  • Reports all threads involved in the cycle
    • 



Definition at line 524 of file debug/mutex.c.


524                                         {
525   mutex_stack_entry_t **all_stacks = NULL;
526   int *stack_counts = NULL;
527   int thread_count = 0;
528 
529   if (mutex_stack_get_all_threads(&all_stacks, &stack_counts, &thread_count) != 0) {
530     return;
531   }
532 
533   // Check each thread for deadlock conditions
534   for (int i = 0; i < thread_count; i++) {
535     thread_lock_stack_t *stack_a = g_thread_registry[i].stack;
536     uintptr_t waiting_for = thread_waiting_for_mutex(stack_a);
537 
538     if (waiting_for == 0)
539       continue; // Thread not waiting
540 
541     // Same-thread deadlock: thread trying to acquire a mutex it already holds
542     if (thread_holds_mutex(stack_a, waiting_for)) {
543       log_error("%s", colored_string(LOG_COLOR_ERROR, "╔═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗"));
544       log_error("%s", colored_string(LOG_COLOR_ERROR, "║  ⚠️  DEADLOCK DETECTED: Same-thread Recursive Lock  ⚠️  ║"));
545       log_error("%s", colored_string(LOG_COLOR_ERROR, "╚═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝"));
546       log_error("  Thread Address:        0x%lx", (unsigned long)g_thread_registry[i].thread_id);
547       log_error("  Mutex:                 0x%lx", waiting_for);
548       log_error("  Issue:                 Thread attempts recursive lock on non-recursive mutex");
549       continue;
550     }
551 
552     // Multi-thread circular wait: use DFS to detect cycles
553     int cycle_path[MAX_CYCLE_LEN];
554     int cycle_len = 0;
555     int cycle_start = detect_cycle_dfs(thread_count, i, cycle_path, &cycle_len);
556 
557     if (cycle_start >= 0 && cycle_len > 1) {
558       // Collect mutexes involved in this deadlock
559       uintptr_t cycle_mutexes[MAX_CYCLE_MUTEXES];
560       int mutex_count = 0;
561       for (int k = 0; k < cycle_len && mutex_count < MAX_CYCLE_MUTEXES; k++) {
562         int thread_idx = cycle_path[k];
563         thread_lock_stack_t *stack = g_thread_registry[thread_idx].stack;
564         uintptr_t waiting_for = thread_waiting_for_mutex(stack);
565         if (waiting_for != 0) {
566           cycle_mutexes[mutex_count++] = waiting_for;
567         }
568       }
569 
570       // Check if mutexes are different from last deadlock
571       bool is_new_deadlock = deadlock_mutexes_changed(cycle_mutexes, mutex_count);
572       if (is_new_deadlock) {
573         update_deadlock_state(cycle_mutexes, mutex_count);
574       }
575 
576       // Cycle detected! Build complete message in one string
577       char cycle_msg[4096];
578       int msg_len = 0;
579 
580       // Leading newline and header box
581       msg_len += snprintf(cycle_msg + msg_len, sizeof(cycle_msg) - msg_len, "\n%s\n",
582                           colored_string(LOG_COLOR_ERROR, "╔═════════════════════════════════╗"));
583       msg_len += snprintf(cycle_msg + msg_len, sizeof(cycle_msg) - msg_len, "%s\n",
584                           colored_string(LOG_COLOR_ERROR, "║  DEADLOCK: Circular Wait Cycle  ║"));
585       msg_len += snprintf(cycle_msg + msg_len, sizeof(cycle_msg) - msg_len, "%s\n",
586                           colored_string(LOG_COLOR_ERROR, "╚═════════════════════════════════╝"));
587 
588       // Print each thread in the cycle
589       for (int k = 0; k < cycle_len; k++) {
590         int thread_idx = cycle_path[k];
591         int next_thread_idx = cycle_path[(k + 1) % cycle_len];
592 
593         thread_lock_stack_t *current_stack = g_thread_registry[thread_idx].stack;
594         uintptr_t current_waiting = thread_waiting_for_mutex(current_stack);
595 
596         char thread_name[256], mutex_name[256], held_by_name[256];
597         NAMED_GET_BY_PTR((uintptr_t)g_thread_registry[thread_idx].thread_id, thread_name, sizeof(thread_name));
598         NAMED_GET_BY_PTR((uintptr_t)current_waiting, mutex_name, sizeof(mutex_name));
599         NAMED_GET_BY_PTR((uintptr_t)g_thread_registry[next_thread_idx].thread_id, held_by_name, sizeof(held_by_name));
600 
601         msg_len += snprintf(cycle_msg + msg_len, sizeof(cycle_msg) - msg_len, "  T%d: %s waits for %s (held by %s)%s",
602                             k + 1, thread_name, mutex_name, held_by_name, k < cycle_len - 1 ? "\n" : "");
603       }
604 
605       // Log repeated deadlock detections (skip first call, throttle subsequent ones)
606       if (!is_new_deadlock) {
607         log_error_every(1000000, "%s", cycle_msg); // 1000000 µs = 1 second
608       }
609     }
610   }
611 
612   mutex_stack_free_all_threads(all_stacks, stack_counts, thread_count);
613 }






References colored_string(), LOG_COLOR_ERROR,
    log_error, log_error_every, MAX_CYCLE_LEN,
    MAX_CYCLE_MUTEXES, mutex_stack_free_all_threads(),
    mutex_stack_get_all_threads(), NAMED_GET_BY_PTR,
    thread_lock_stack_t::stack, and thread_id.






void
  mutex_stack_free_all_threads (mutex_stack_entry_t ** stacks, int *
  stack_counts, int thread_count)


Free memory allocated by mutex_stack_get_all_threads().


Definition at line 347 of file debug/mutex.c.


347                                                                                                      {
348 
349   if (!stacks || !stack_counts)
350     return;
351 
352   for (int i = 0; i < thread_count; i++) {
353     SAFE_FREE(stacks[i]);
354   }
355 
356   SAFE_FREE(stacks);
357   SAFE_FREE(stack_counts);
358 }

int
  mutex_stack_get_all_threads (mutex_stack_entry_t *** out_stacks, int **
  out_stack_counts, int * out_thread_count)


Get all threads' lock stacks for deadlock analysis. Allocates
    memory for all thread stacks. Caller must free with
    mutex_stack_free_all_threads().




Parameters


out_stacks Pointer to array of stacks (one per
  thread)


out_stack_counts Array of stack sizes corresponding to out_stacks


out_thread_count Number of threads with lock stacks


Returns


0 on success, -1 on error


Definition at line 284 of file debug/mutex.c.


284                                                                                                                   {
285 
286   if (!out_stacks || !out_stack_counts || !out_thread_count) {
287     return -1;
288   }
289 
290   // Read the thread count with acquire semantics (lock-free, no mutex needed)
291   // Use memory_order_seq_cst to ensure we get a consistent snapshot
292   int thread_count = atomic_load_int(&g_thread_registry_count);
293 
294   // Allocate arrays for threads in the registry
295   // Note: SAFE_MALLOC takes bytes as first parameter, not count
296   *out_stacks = SAFE_MALLOC(thread_count * sizeof(mutex_stack_entry_t *), mutex_stack_entry_t **);
297   *out_stack_counts = SAFE_MALLOC(thread_count * sizeof(int), int *);
298 
299   if (!*out_stacks || !*out_stack_counts) {
300     SAFE_FREE(*out_stacks);
301     SAFE_FREE(*out_stack_counts);
302     return -1;
303   }
304 
305   // Copy each thread's stack from the registry
306   // Note: the thread count can change concurrently, so we re-read it in the loop
307   // to avoid accessing out-of-bounds memory if threads exit during iteration
308   int actual_count = 0;
309   for (int i = 0; i < thread_count; i++) {
310     // Re-check thread count in case registry shrank
311     int current_registry_count = atomic_load_int(&g_thread_registry_count);
312     if (i >= current_registry_count) {
313       break;
314     }
315 
316     thread_lock_stack_t *src = g_thread_registry[i].stack;
317     if (!src) {
318       // Stack not yet initialized for this thread, skip it
319       (*out_stack_counts)[i] = 0;
320       (*out_stacks)[i] = NULL;
321       continue;
322     }
323 
324     // Defensively get depth - it could change or be freed by another thread
325     int depth = 0;
326     if (src && src->depth >= 0 && src->depth < MUTEX_STACK_MAX_DEPTH) {
327       depth = src->depth;
328     }
329 
330     (*out_stack_counts)[i] = depth;
331 
332     if (depth > 0) {
333       (*out_stacks)[i] = SAFE_MALLOC(depth * sizeof(mutex_stack_entry_t), mutex_stack_entry_t *);
334       if ((*out_stacks)[i]) {
335         memcpy((*out_stacks)[i], src->stack, depth * sizeof(mutex_stack_entry_t));
336         actual_count++;
337       }
338     } else {
339       (*out_stacks)[i] = NULL;
340     }
341   }
342 
343   *out_thread_count = actual_count;
344   return 0;
345 }






References atomic_load_int(),
    thread_lock_stack_t::depth, MUTEX_STACK_MAX_DEPTH,
    SAFE_FREE, SAFE_MALLOC, and
  thread_lock_stack_t::stack.


Referenced by mutex_stack_detect_deadlocks().






int
  mutex_stack_get_current (mutex_stack_entry_t * out_entries, int
  max_entries)


Get the current thread's lock stack.


Parameters


out_entries Pointer to array where entries will be
  stored


max_entries Maximum number of entries to return


Returns


Number of entries in the stack (may be > max_entries
  if truncated)


Definition at line 269 of file debug/mutex.c.


269                                                                                {
270   thread_lock_stack_t *stack = get_thread_local_stack();
271   if (!stack || !out_entries) {
272     return 0;
273   }
274 
275   int count = (stack->depth < max_entries) ? stack->depth : max_entries;
276   memcpy(out_entries, stack->stack, count * sizeof(mutex_stack_entry_t));
277   return stack->depth; // Return actual depth even if truncated
278 }






References thread_lock_stack_t::depth, and
    thread_lock_stack_t::stack.






int
  mutex_stack_init (void )


Initialize mutex stack system.


Returns


0 on success, -1 on error


Definition at line 706 of file debug/mutex.c.


706                            {
707   // No initialization needed - registry uses lock-free atomic operations
708   return 0;
709 }










void
  mutex_stack_mark_locked (uintptr_t mutex_key)


Mark the top of the current thread's lock stack as LOCKED
    (transitions PENDING -> LOCKED).


Parameters


mutex_key Pointer to mutex (must match most recent
  pending push)


Definition at line 236 of file debug/mutex.c.


236                                                   {
237   thread_lock_stack_t *stack = get_thread_local_stack();
238   if (!stack || stack->depth == 0) {
239     return;
240   }
241 
242   // Mark the top of the stack as locked
243   int top = stack->depth - 1;
244   if (stack->stack[top].mutex_key == mutex_key) {
245     stack->stack[top].state = MUTEX_STACK_STATE_LOCKED;
246     stack->stack[top].timestamp_ns = time_get_ns();
247   }
248 
249   // Thread-local only. Registry is populated on-demand by mutex_stack_get_all_threads()
250 }






References thread_lock_stack_t::depth,
    mutex_stack_entry_t::mutex_key, MUTEX_STACK_STATE_LOCKED,
    thread_lock_stack_t::stack, mutex_stack_entry_t::state,
    time_get_ns(), and mutex_stack_entry_t::timestamp_ns.






void
  mutex_stack_pop (uintptr_t mutex_key)


Pop the top mutex from the current thread's lock stack
  (unlock).


Parameters


mutex_key Pointer to mutex (used for
  validation)


Definition at line 252 of file debug/mutex.c.


252                                           {
253   thread_lock_stack_t *stack = get_thread_local_stack();
254   if (!stack || stack->depth == 0) {
255     return;
256   }
257 
258   // Validate top matches
259   int top = stack->depth - 1;
260   if (stack->stack[top].mutex_key == mutex_key) {
261     stack->depth--;
262   }
263 
264   // Thread-local only. Registry is populated on-demand by mutex_stack_get_all_threads()
265 }






References thread_lock_stack_t::depth,
    mutex_stack_entry_t::mutex_key, and
    thread_lock_stack_t::stack.






void
  mutex_stack_push_pending (uintptr_t mutex_key, const char *
  mutex_name)


Push a mutex onto the current thread's lock stack (PENDING
  state).


Parameters


mutex_key Pointer to mutex (used as unique ID)


mutex_name Human-readable name of mutex


Definition at line 220 of file debug/mutex.c.


220                                                                            {
221   thread_lock_stack_t *stack = get_thread_local_stack();
222   if (!stack || stack->depth >= MUTEX_STACK_MAX_DEPTH) {
223     return;
224   }
225 
226   // Register this thread in the global registry on first mutex use
227   register_thread_if_needed();
228 
229   stack->stack[stack->depth].mutex_key = mutex_key;
230   stack->stack[stack->depth].mutex_name = mutex_name;
231   stack->stack[stack->depth].state = MUTEX_STACK_STATE_PENDING;
232   stack->stack[stack->depth].timestamp_ns = time_get_ns();
233   stack->depth++;
234 }






References thread_lock_stack_t::depth,
    mutex_stack_entry_t::mutex_key,
    mutex_stack_entry_t::mutex_name, MUTEX_STACK_MAX_DEPTH,
    MUTEX_STACK_STATE_PENDING, thread_lock_stack_t::stack,
    mutex_stack_entry_t::state, time_get_ns(), and
    mutex_stack_entry_t::timestamp_ns.
